problem: In the batch report's "Decision-oriented top actions" table, each row's Impact cell ("Fixes N occurrences...") and Reach cell (occurrence_count) describe the same underlying finding but disagree by an order of magnitude, because expected_impact_kind is cloned from a single representative underlying finding's own small per-instance occurrence_count, while the row's own occurrence_count/Reach column is the correctly batch-aggregated acc.count.
evidence: Found by the report-critic skill's dry-run eval against reports/casoon-batch-en301549.json: every top_actions row showed a mismatch (contrast: Impact cites 12 occurrences vs Reach column showing 190; "Missing semantic structure": 2 vs 32; "Content clipped...": 1 vs 3) — traced to src/output/builder/batch.rs around line 1272 (expected_impact_kind: finding.expected_impact_kind.clone()), where the per-instance and the aggregated occurrence counts are never reconciled after aggregation.
expected: A top_actions row's Impact-cell occurrence claim and its Reach-cell occurrence_count must describe the same aggregation scope (both per-instance or both batch-aggregated), not one of each.
counter_examples: A single-report top action (no cross-page aggregation happening) is not subject to this mismatch by construction and must not be flagged.
